name: NAD+
subtitle: Essential Cellular Coenzyme & Anti-Aging Therapy
overview: NAD+ is a crucial coenzyme found in every cell, involved in energy generation and cellular maintenance that naturally declines with age. Supplementation may support cellular health, boost energy, enhance cognitive function, and promote longevity through multiple delivery methods.
mechanism: Direct delivery to bloodstream ensures maximum cellular availability for energy production via ATP synthesis, DNA repair through PARP activation, and sirtuin-mediated longevity pathways. Supports mitochondrial function and metabolic homeostasis.
keyBenefits: Restores mitochondrial function and ATP synthesis

items.name: Cellular Energy Production
items.description: Restores NAD+ levels for optimal ATP synthesis

items.name: DNA Repair
items.description: Activates PARP enzymes for genomic stability

deliveryMethods.overview: Highest bioavailability (100%) via IV, IM, or subcutaneous injection.
NAD+ precursors (NMN, NR) bypass digestive degradation and convert via salvage pathways.
Direct brain access via olfactory and trigeminal pathways.

interactions.notes: Enhanced cellular repair and energy production
Supports metabolic pathways enhanced by GLP-1 agonists
Growth hormone pathways benefit from improved energy metabolism
Alcohol significantly reduces NAD+ levels and effectiveness

timeline.effects: Initial energy improvements and mental clarity
Sustained energy levels and improved sleep quality
Optimized metabolic function and cognitive benefits

stopSigns: Severe injection site reactions or infection signs

bad: Cloudy or discolored solution indicates contamination
Room temperature storage degrades NAD+ rapidly

references.title: NAD+ and Sirtuins in Aging and Disease
references.keyFindings: Seminal review establishing that NAD+ decline is central to aging, driving defects in sirtuin-mediated nuclear and mitochondrial functions and resulting in age-associated pathologies.

references.title: Chronic Nicotinamide Riboside Supplementation Is Well-Tolerated and Elevates NAD+ in Healthy Middle-Aged and Older Adults
references.keyFindings: Randomized crossover trial showing NR (1000 mg/day for 6 weeks) is well tolerated and effectively elevates NAD+ in healthy middle-aged and older adults, with preliminary evidence for reduced blood pressure and arterial stiffness.

references.title: Nicotinamide Mononucleotide Increases Muscle Insulin Sensitivity in Prediabetic Women
references.keyFindings: First human clinical trial showing NMN (250 mg/day for 10 weeks) increases skeletal muscle insulin signaling and sensitivity in overweight/obese postmenopausal women with prediabetes.

references.title: NAD+ Metabolism and Its Roles in Cellular Processes During Ageing
references.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of NAD+ metabolism during aging, detailing how senescent cells drive NAD+ decline via CD38+ macrophage activation and outlining therapeutic restoration strategies.

references.title: Efficacy of Oral NMN Supplementation on Glucose and Lipid Metabolism: A Systematic Review with Meta-Analysis of RCTs
references.keyFindings: Meta-analysis of 8 RCTs (342 adults) found NMN significantly elevates blood NAD+ levels, but short-term supplementation (250-2000 mg/d) did not show significant improvements in glucose control or lipid profile in the general population.

references.title: A Randomized Placebo-Controlled Trial of Nicotinamide Riboside in Older Adults with Mild Cognitive Impairment
references.keyFindings: NR was well tolerated and significantly increased blood NAD+ concentrations in older adults with MCI. Global methylation analyses indicated modest NR-associated reduction in epigenetic age by PhenoAge and GrimAge clocks.

latestResearch.title: NR and NAD+ in Long-COVID: Randomized Controlled Trial
latestResearch.summary: Double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of NR (2000 mg/day) in 58 long-COVID participants evaluating NAD+ levels, cognition, fatigue, sleep quality, and mood symptoms over 24 weeks.

latestResearch.title: NR Benefits in Werner Syndrome (Accelerated Aging)
latestResearch.summary: Double-blind randomized crossover trial showing NR supplementation benefits in patients with Werner syndrome, a genetic accelerated aging disorder.

latestResearch.title: NMN and NR Effects on Skeletal Muscle: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
latestResearch.source: Journal of Cachexia, Sarcopenia and Muscle
latestResearch.summary: Meta-analysis evaluating NMN and NR supplementation effects on skeletal muscle mass and function in adults over 60, with mixed results on clinical outcomes despite NAD+ elevation.

quickStats.typicalDose: IV: 250-1000mg; IM/SubQ: 100-500mg; Intranasal: 25-50mg; Oral precursors: 100-500mg NMN/NR
quickStats.frequency: IV: 1-2x weekly; IM/SubQ: 2-3x weekly; Intranasal: 1-2x daily; Oral: daily
quickStats.cycleDuration: 4-12 weeks; IV therapy requires medical supervision
